content_markdown: Which of the following would **not** reduce the effects of the enhanced greenhouse effect?

options.label: A
options.text: Replacing natural gas power stations with those using oil

options.label: B
options.text: Replacing conventional vehicles with hybrid vehicles

options.label: C
options.text: Replacing fossil fuel power stations with those using nuclear fuel

options.label: D
options.text: Increasing the use of carbon dioxide capture and storage

vibe_explanation: The enhanced greenhouse effect is primarily driven by $CO_2$ emissions. Natural gas is the 'cleanest' fossil fuel in terms of $CO_2$ emitted per unit of energy produced. Switching from natural gas to oil (which has a higher carbon content) would actually increase $CO_2$ emissions, thereby worsening rather than reducing the enhanced greenhouse effect. Options B, C, and D all describe methods that either reduce $CO_2$ production or remove it from the atmosphere.
